model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Vanderbilt enters the tournament as an analytical powerhouse, ranking in the top 12 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik thanks to the nation's 6th-most efficient offense. Led by the dynamic scoring of Tyler Tanner and Duke Miles, the Commodores possess a level of shot-making and SEC-tested athleticism that should overwhelm a McNeese squad ranked outside the top 60. While McNeese is a disciplined mid-major with a balanced scoring attack, they lack the defensive interior presence to consistently stop Vanderbilt's high-powered transition and half-court sets.

reasoningStep.title: Vanderbilt vs. McNeese Matchup Evaluation
reasoningStep.summary: Vanderbilt's elite offensive efficiency and top-15 analytical profile make them heavy favorites against a solid but outmatched McNeese team.
evidence: Vanderbilt ranks #12 in KenPom and #10 in Torvik with the #6 adjusted offensive efficiency nationally.
McNeese ranks #67 in KenPom, showing a significant gap in overall efficiency margin (28.12 vs 12.52).
Vanderbilt's Tyler Tanner (19.1 PPG) and Duke Miles (16.5 PPG) provide high-major scoring depth that McNeese's 101st-ranked defense will struggle to contain.
Vanderbilt is battle-tested from a difficult SEC schedule, including a recent 91-74 win over Florida in the conference tournament.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
